retain: Tile-group segment metadata is still useful for the final compact reducer and gives the desired 12.34x to 23.81x write-reduction envelope.
The 32x512 segment distribution remains attractive for a reducer: p50 segment length is 23 and p90/p99 are 32.
reject: A group-owned segment CTA as the contribution producer. Every measured segment has at most one selected row per token, so a segment-owned producer cannot naturally reuse one token's q/dO across a K16 selected block.
A segment-owned MFMA rewrite that computes one group segment directly; it would lose the query-major KxH by HxD reuse and repeat the scalar/persistent-CTA S4096 failure mode.
decision.next_kernel_requirement: Keep token/query-major MFMA contribution production, then perform cross-token/group segmented reduction with warp/block cooperation or emit compact rows for the already-positive rocPRIM reducer. Do not move the producer owner to compressed-group segments unless the kernel explicitly preserves token-local q/dO reuse.
decision.read: The new token-reuse diagnostic explains why the attractive 23.81x route-segment write reduction has not translated into S4096 latency wins: sorting by compressed group creates good ScatterAdd segments but destroys the same-token selected-K reuse that makes the fast dot-K producer efficient.
